perturbation.original_text: “Confidenti al Information” is defined as: CEII made available through a restricted area within the GTC’s OASIS website or otherwise furnished to you by GTC; (ii) new information created using such information and that contains CEII, such as any copies, summaries, di agrams, notes, calculations, recommendations, evaluations, opinions, reports and/or conclusions or other similar materials prepared from or based upon the Confidential Information, including any such information that has been stored electronically (“Deriva tive Information”); and (iii) any information you know or reasonably should know to be CEII that is furnished and/or made available to you by GTC in connection with this Non -Disclosure Agreement. Confidential Information would include, but would not be lim ited to, load flow base case models and/or stability models, designs, formulae, processes, models, photographs, plans, drawings, schematics, sketches, samples, equipment, equipment performance reports, GTC bidders’ lists, pricing information, studies, repo rts, findings, inventions, ideas, specifications, parts lists, technical data, data bases, computer programs, except those computer programs proprietary to Recipient , flow charts, algorithms, information regarding GTC’s executives and employees, and other business and technical information which are used for purposes of any project performed by GTC for Recipient .
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidenti al Information” is defined as: CEII made available through a restricted area within the GTC’s OASIS website or otherwise furnished to you by GTC; (ii) new information created using such information and that contains CEII, such as any copies, summaries, di agrams, notes, calculations, recommendations, evaluations, opinions, reports and/or conclusions or other similar materials prepared from or based upon the Confidential Information, including any such information that has been stored electronically (“Deriva tive Information”); and (iii) any information you know or reasonably should know to be CEII that is furnished and/or made available to you by GTC in connection with this Non -Disclosure Agreement. Confidential Information would include, but would not be lim ited to, designs, formulae, processes, models, photographs, plans, drawings, schematics, sketches, samples, equipment, equipment performance reports, GTC bidders’ lists, pricing information, studies, repo rts, findings, inventions, ideas, specifications, parts lists, technical data, data bases, computer programs, except those computer programs proprietary to Recipient , flow charts, algorithms, information regarding GTC’s executives and employees, and other business and technical information which are used for purposes of any project performed by GTC for Recipient .
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'load flow base case models and/or stability models' from the definition of Confidential Information, it creates an in-text contradiction if Section II refers to Recipient requesting access to 'load flow base case models'. Now the contract could be interpreted to mean these models are not protected under the NDA since they're not in the definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient desires access to information of a proprietary and confidential nature relating to (describe CEII and Confidential Information requested) __________________ NDA Version 1 Initials: ________________ Page 2 of 7 Date : ________________ ______ __________________ ________________________________ ______________ __ for the intend us e (describe): ____________________________ ____________ ________ ________________________________________________________________________
perturbation.justification: The change removes 'load flow base case models and/or stability models' from the definition of Confidential Information. The contradicted text is a fill-in-the-blank section for describing 'CEII and Confidential Information requested'. If the recipient requests 'load flow base case models', the changed text creates a contradiction because the models are no longer explicitly defined as Confidential Information, which creates uncertainty about whether they are protected under the NDA, fulfilling the condition for an Omissions-In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information solely to : (a) those individuals within your organization who (i ) have requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non - disclosure; and (iv) have executed this Non-Disclosure Agreement. Additional persons employed by your organization may execute this Agreement using the form set forth in Appendix A (b) personnel hired by you for help and/or advice, such as business consultants, lawyers, and/or accountants, if those personnel (i) ha ve individually requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non -disclosure; and (iv) have individually executed this Non Disclosure Agreement . Such personnel must execute this Agreement in the form set forth in Appendix B.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information solely to : (a) those individuals within your organization who (i ) have requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non - disclosure; and (iv) have executed this Non-Disclosure Agreement. Additional persons employed by your organization may execute this Agreement using the form set forth in Appendix A (b) personnel hired by you for help and/or advice, such as business consultants, lawyers, and/or accountants, if those personnel (i) ha ve individually requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non -disclosure; and (iv) have individually executed this Non Disclosure Agreement .
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'Such personnel must execute this Agreement in the form set forth in Appendix B', it contradicts the clause that states 'have individually executed this Non Disclosure Agreement', creating uncertainty about how the Non Disclosure Agreement should be executed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information solely to : (a) those individuals within your organization who (i ) have requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non - disclosure; and (iv) have executed this Non-Disclosure Agreement. Additional persons employed by your organization may execute this Agreement using the form set forth in Appendix A (b) personnel hired by you for help and/or advice, such as business consultants, lawyers, and/or accountants, if those personnel (i) ha ve individually requested access to Confidential Information from GTC and have been granted such access; (ii) need to know the information to do their jobs; (iii) have been advised of the duty of non -disclosure; and (iv) have individually executed this Non Disclosure Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the requirement 'Such personnel must execute this Agreement in the form set forth in Appendix B', while the contradicted text states that personnel hired for advice must execute the Non-Disclosure Agreement. The omission creates uncertainty and potential contradiction because it's no longer explicitly clear which form (Appendix A or some other form) should be used by the personnel hired for help and/or advice. This means that the removal in the changed text creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees that you will only use Confidential Information solely for the purpose as stated in Section II. You may not use Confidential Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees that you will only use Confidential Information for the purpose stated in Section II. You may not use Confidential Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'solely' from 'for the purpose as stated in Section II' creates ambiguity. Does this mean the recipient can use Confidential Information for the stated purpose and additional purposes, contradicting 'You may not use Confidential Information for any other purpose.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient agrees that you will only use Confidential Information for the purpose stated in Section II. You may not use Confidential Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the recipient must use confidential information 'solely' for the purpose in Section II, preventing them from using it for any other reason. The changed text removes 'solely', now implying that the recipient can use confidential information for the purpose in Section II, but potentially also for other purposes as well. This contradicts the explicit prohibition of using Confidential Information for any other purpose in both the Changed Text and Contradicted Text, therefore the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations imposed on either party herein shall not apply to Confidential Information which: A. Is made public by GTC or which otherwise becomes part of the public domain through no wrongful act, fault or negligence on the part of the Recipient or its employees; B. The Recipient can reasonably demonstrate is already in the Recipient ’s possession as a result of matters unrelated to the Project and not subject to an agreement of confidentialit y; C. Is received from a third party without restriction and without breach of an agreement with GTC; D. Is independently developed by the Recipient without reliance upon and not as result of information received in connection with any work performed f or GTC, or any Confidential information received, as evidenced by the Recipient ’s written records; E. Is independently developed by the Recipient for purposes unrelated to the Project, as evidenced by the Recipient ’s written records; or F. The Recipient is required to disclose pursuant to a valid order of a court, governmental body or regulatory agency, provided that to the extent it may lawfully do so, the Recipient shall first have given written notice to GTC and given GTC a reasonable opportunity to interpose an objection or obtain a protective order , and to cooperate with GTC in taking steps to restrict or narrow the information you must disclose.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations imposed on either party herein shall not apply to Confidential Information which: A. Is made public by GTC or which otherwise becomes part of the public domain through no wrongful act, fault or negligence on the part of the Recipient or its employees; B. The Recipient can reasonably demonstrate is already in the Recipient ’s possession as a result of matters unrelated to the Project and not subject to an agreement of confidentialit y; C. Is received from a third party without restriction and without breach of an agreement with GTC; D. Is independently developed by the Recipient without reliance upon and not as result of information received in connection with any work performed f or GTC, or any Confidential information received, as evidenced by the Recipient ’s written records; E. Is independently developed by the Recipient for purposes unrelated to the Project; or F. The Recipient is required to disclose pursuant to a valid order of a court, governmental body or regulatory agency, provided that to the extent it may lawfully do so, the Recipient shall first have given written notice to GTC and given GTC a reasonable opportunity to interpose an objection or obtain a protective order , and to cooperate with GTC in taking steps to restrict or narrow the information you must disclose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'as evidenced by the Recipient’s written records' from Section E, it creates an in-text contradiction. Previously independent development needed written record evidence; now, the lack of this requirement contradicts the higher standard required in Section D, where written records are still necessary to claim independent development.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations imposed on either party herein shall not apply to Confidential Information which: A. Is made public by GTC or which otherwise becomes part of the public domain through no wrongful act, fault or negligence on the part of the Recipient or its employees; B. The Recipient can reasonably demonstrate is already in the Recipient ’s possession as a result of matters unrelated to the Project and not subject to an agreement of confidentialit y; C. Is received from a third party without restriction and without breach of an agreement with GTC; D. Is independently developed by the Recipient without reliance upon and not as result of information received in connection with any work performed f or GTC, or any Confidential information received, as evidenced by the Recipient ’s written records; E. Is independently developed by the Recipient for purposes unrelated to the Project; or F. The Recipient is required to disclose pursuant to a valid order of a court, governmental body or regulatory agency, provided that to the extent it may lawfully do so, the Recipient shall first have given written notice to GTC and given GTC a reasonable opportunity to interpose an objection or obtain a protective order , and to cooperate with GTC in taking steps to restrict or narrow the information you must disclose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for 'written records' as evidence for independent development in Section E, while Section D still requires it. This creates a contradiction because the standard of proof for independent development now differs between sections, impacting the enforceability and interpretation of 'Confidential Information' exceptions. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text and the Original Text is slightly different than both.

perturbation.original_text: This Non -Disclosure Agreement shall expire on three (3) calendar years from December 31 of the year in which you execute this No n-Disclosure Agreement; provided, however, that notwithstanding the termination of this Non -Disclosure Agreement, the confidentiality obligations and covenants of this Non -Disclosure Agreement will survive indefinitely and shall not terminate, or else shal l remain in effect as long as permitted by law. GTC may revoke your access to Confidential Information at any time, with or without notice . GTC may extend the term of this Non -Disclosure Agreement in its sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: This Non -Disclosure Agreement shall expire on three (3) calendar years from December 31 of the year in which you execute this No n-Disclosure Agreement; provided, however, that notwithstanding the termination of this Non -Disclosure Agreement, the confidentiality obligations and covenants of this Non -Disclosure Agreement will survive indefinitely and shall not terminate, or else shal l remain in effect as long as permitted by law. GTC may revoke your access to Confidential Information at any time, with or without notice .
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'GTC may extend the term of this Non -Disclosure Agreement in its sole discretion.', the agreement now states that the NDA expires after three years, and the confidentiality obligations survive indefinitely. The absence of an extension clause introduces uncertainty and contradicts the prior stated expiry of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non -Disclosure Agreement shall expire on three (3) calendar years from December 31 of the year in which you execute this No n-Disclosure Agreement; provided, however, that notwithstanding the termination of this Non -Disclosure Agreement, the confidentiality obligations and covenants of this Non -Disclosure Agreement will survive indefinitely and shall not terminate, or else shal l remain in effect as long as permitted by law. GTC may revoke your access to Confidential Information at any time, with or without notice .
perturbation.justification: The original text included a clause allowing GTC to extend the NDA's term, which provided a mechanism to reconcile the initial 3-year expiry with the indefinite survival of confidentiality obligations. The changed text removes this extension clause. The contradicted text and the changed text are in the same place, but the absence of the extension clause creates a strong contradiction, as the NDA is stated to expire after three years, but the confidentiality obligations are said to last indefinitely. Without the extension clause, it's unclear how the confidentiality obligations can survive indefinitely when the agreement itself has a definite end date.
